technical_documents.description: An off-grid, zero-water ecological sanitation (EcoSan) infrastructure blueprint. Utilizing source-separated source diversion hydraulics paired with an integrated solar-updraft ventilation flue, this system isolates sterile urine from organic solid waste, driving rapid desiccation and thermodynamic decomposition to generate pathogen-free, agricultural-grade bio-fertilizer with zero utility overhead.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Over 2.4 billion people globally lack access to safe, climate-resilient containment sanitation. Traditional unlined pit latrines continuously leach hazardous nitrates and fecal pathogens into shallow shared water tables, while conventional water-flush systems consume over 30% of total domestic potable water volumes—rendering both operational archetypes unsustainable in water-scarce or developing rural communities.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The design deploys an advanced dual-drainage mechanical urine-diverting pedestal that isolates sterile liquid fractions (accounting for 80% of aggregate human waste volume) via gravity drainage into a localized collection vessel for agricultural nitrogen reuse. Concurrently, solid organic matter drops into an isolated, sealed twin-vault dehydration containment chamber. Regular applications of high-carbon desiccant cover media (fine sawdust) drop relative humidity metrics below 25%. Combined with passive continuous extraction from a high-emissivity black-coated solar updraft chimney flue, this thermal configuration accelerates complete pathogen attenuation and organic stabilization within a 12-month processing cycle without active mechanical components.

bill_of_materials.name: Ergonomic Urine-Diverting Dual-Drainage Ceramic Pedestal Intermediary
Heavy-Duty Food-Grade HDPE Liquid Collection Jerry Can (20L Capacity)
Marine-Grade Sealed Exterior Plywood Dehydration Chamber Box Assembly
Rigid UV-Stabilized PVC Updraft Vent Flue Pipe (75mm Diameter, 3m Length)
High-Carbon Sifted Agro-Forestry Sawdust Cover Media Stock

specifications.separation_mechanism: Source-separated urine diversion fluidics with solar-updraft ventilation

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Build the dehydration chamber from plywood with a hinged front access door; seal all joints.
Step 2: Install the urine-diverting pedestal on top; connect urine outlet to jerry can via flexible hose.
Step 3: Run the 3m PVC vent pipe from the chamber up through the roof; cap with a fly screen.
Step 4: Paint the vent pipe black to create a solar-driven updraft that continuously extracts moisture.
Step 5: After each use, add one cup of sawdust; empty urine can weekly (dilute 10:1 for garden fertiliser); rotate chamber bins every 6 months.

comments.text: No smell, no water, and free fertiliser. Installed in 5 households in our community.
